headline: Review 1
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Management is completely disconnected from what engineers actually do. Directors micromanage every decision and there is no trust.

headline: Review 2
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Senior leadership changes priorities every quarter. It is impossible to ship anything because strategy keeps shifting.

headline: Review 3
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: My manager takes credit for the team's work in every review cycle. Feedback is never constructive.

headline: Review 4
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Too many layers of management. Getting a simple decision approved takes weeks and multiple escalations.

headline: Review 5
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Leadership is toxic. Managers yell in meetings and nobody in HR seems to care.

headline: Review 6
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Management promotes based on politics not performance. If you are not in the right clique you will not advance.

headline: Review 7
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Directors have no technical background yet override every engineering decision. Very frustrating.

headline: Review 8
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Pay is way below market for the Bay Area. I got a 2% raise after a strong performance review which is insulting.

headline: Review 9
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Compensation has not kept up with inflation. Colleagues at competitors earn 40% more for the same role.

headline: Review 10
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Equity refreshes are tiny compared to what is offered at other big tech companies.

headline: Review 11
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Bonuses are discretionary and almost always below target. The total comp story they tell during recruiting is misleading.

headline: Review 12
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: No salary bands are disclosed. You only find out you are underpaid when a new hire gets more than you.

headline: Review 13
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Benefits have been cut three years in a row. Health insurance coverage is now worse than most competitors.

headline: Review 14
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Constant on-call rotations with no compensation or time off in lieu. Burnout is a real problem here.

headline: Review 15
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Expected to be available on weekends and evenings. Work-life balance is a myth at this company.

headline: Review 16
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Headcount keeps shrinking but the work keeps growing. The team is stretched way too thin.

headline: Review 17
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: The culture glorifies overwork. If you leave at 6pm people assume you are not committed.

headline: Review 18
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Product deadlines are always unrealistic. Crunch mode is the default not the exception.

headline: Review 19
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: No real PTO culture. People are pressured to stay connected even when officially on vacation.

headline: Review 20
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Meetings all day with no heads-down focus time. Impossible to actually get work done during business hours.

headline: Review 21
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Promotion criteria are never clearly explained. I have been at the same level for three years with no path forward.

headline: Review 22
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: There is no internal mobility program. If you want a new role you basically have to re-interview from scratch.

headline: Review 23
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Training budget is practically zero. You are expected to grow on your own time and own dime.

headline: Review 24
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: The org is very flat at senior levels so there is a ceiling you hit very quickly.

headline: Review 25
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Performance reviews are a black box. You never know how you are actually being assessed.

headline: Review 26
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Career ladder documentation is outdated and managers interpret it differently. There is no consistency.

headline: Review 27
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Pay is decent but management issues and lack of growth make it hard to stay motivated.

headline: Review 28
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: Great brand name but the actual day-to-day is exhausting. Work-life balance is terrible and salary is stagnant.

headline: Review 29
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: I liked my team but upper management is out of touch and there is no path to promotion.

headline: Review 30
pros: Good brand name and smart colleagues.
cons: The company is coasting on its reputation. Internal tools are broken, processes are slow, and pay is falling behind.
